channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jackie Matubia and Ex-Husband Celebrate Daughter Zari's 11th Birthday with Intimate Backyard Gathering
item.description: Jackie Matubia, renowned Kenyan actress, celebrated her daughter Zari's 11th birthday with a low-key backyard gathering alongside her ex-husband Kennedy Njogu. The event, shaped entirely by Zari's pre...

item.title: No Winners in PCSO Major Lotto Draws on July 20, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that there were no winners for both the Grand Lotto 6/55 and Megalotto 6/45 jackpot prizes on Monday, July 20, 2026. The Grand Lotto 6/55 jac...

item.title: Sir Garfield Sobers: A Tribute to the Legendary All-Rounder
item.description: Sir Garfield Sobers, widely regarded as one of the greatest all-rounders in cricket history, has passed away at the age of 89, marking the end of an era for the sport. Tributes have flooded in from fa...

item.title: Kolkata Chai Collaborates with Bungalow Hospitality for Business Partnership
item.description: The article discusses the strategic partnership between Kolkata Chai, a renowned tea brand, and Bungalow Hospitality, a hospitality management company. The collaboration aims to expand their market re...

item.title: Jersey Mike's Sevaluates at $8 Billion Amid Competitive Restaurant Market
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that Jersey Mike's, a prominent pizza chain, has garnered significant attention for its projected valuation of $8 billion. This figure far exceeds the valuations of oth...

item.title: Man killed, wife injured in home invasion incident in Imphal East
item.description: A 52-year-old man and his 53-year-old wife were attacked in their Imphal East home by unknown assailants around 2:30 am. Police confirmed that the couple was shot and stabbed after intruders broke int...

item.title: U.S. military confirms two soldiers killed in Jordan by Iranian strikes, first since war began
item.description: The U.S. military has confirmed the deaths of two soldiers in Jordan following Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks, marking the first direct casualties from Iranian fire since the conflict wit...

item.title: The Art of Knolling: Organized Objects in Design and Daily Life
item.description: The article explores the concept of knolling, a minimalist arrangement technique that organizes objects in precise, parallel formations. Originating from Andrew Kromelow's practice of arranging tools ...

item.title: Naheed Nenshi apologizes for photo with Israeli ambassador, highlighting tensions within Alberta NDP
item.description: In a recent opinion piece, Calgary-based commentator Rob Breakenridge discusses the controversy surrounding Alberta NDP Leader Naheed Nenshi. The issue arose after Nenshi was photographed with Israel'...

item.title: U.S. Gas Prices Exceed $4 Per Gallon for Second Consecutive Week
item.description: The U.S. average gas price has surpassed $4 per gallon for the second consecutive week, marking a significant increase from previous months. According to the latest data from the U.S. Energy Informati...

item.title: The View hosts criticise Trump for intruding on Spain's World Cup victory celebration
item.description: The View hosts have condemned Donald Trump for his uninvited appearance at Spain's World Cup victory celebrations, criticising his attempt to join the team's photo with the trophy. During the World Cu...

item.title: Lilian Muli Shares Heartwarming Birthday Video of 16-Year-Old Son with iPhone Gift
item.description: Citizen TV anchor Lilian Muli has shared a heartfelt birthday video of her 16-year-old son Joshua, revealing a rare glimpse of his private life. In the clip, Lilian surprises Joshua with a handwritten...

item.title: Tanzanian Students Demand Stricter Penalties for FGM Practitioners
item.description: Female students from secondary schools in Serengeti and Bunda districts are urging the government to introduce stricter penalties, including capital punishment by hanging, for those involved in female...

item.title: Billionaire cousins evict LGBTQ club from Sydney church, sparking legal dispute
item.description: Billionaire cousins Anthony El-Hazouri and Charbel Hazzouri, co-founders of commercial landlord Revelop, have terminated the lease of an LGBTQ+ nightclub, Heaps Gay Events, from a deconsecrated church...

item.title: South Africa's Police Task Team Secures Trial for Three High-Profile Cases
item.description: The Acting National Police Commissioner, Lieutenant General Puleng Dimpane, has praised the Political Killings Task Team (PKTT) and Gauteng Counter Intelligence Operations (GCI-OPS) for their role in ...

item.title: Exploring Urban Loneliness and Economic Growth in India
item.description: The article delves into the growing phenomenon of urban loneliness amidst rapid economic development in India. It highlights how cities are becoming hubs for economic opportunities but also breeding g...

item.title: Clashes between BIFF remnants and security forces in Maguindanao del Norte result in casualties
item.description: A violent clash between armed remnants of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF) and security forces in Maguindanao del Norte resulted in multiple casualties and injuries. The incident, which ...

item.title: Study shows sugary drink tax in Egypt could cut disease and save healthcare costs
item.description: A new study suggests imposing a tax on sugary drinks in Egypt could significantly reduce the prevalence of lifestyle-related diseases like diabetes and obesity, while also saving the country billions ...

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau's Penalty Dispute Causes Tournament Delay Involving Trump
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau, a prominent LIV Golf player, caused a significant delay at The Open Championship by disputing a two-stroke penalty imposed by the Royal and Ancient Golf Club of St Andrews. The inci...

item.title: US Embassy in Namibia to Shift Visa Services to South African Hubs
item.description: The United States Department of State has announced significant changes to visa services for Namibians, effective 1 August 2026. The US embassy in Namibia will no longer provide visa services for citi...
